cardiometabolic_tracker v0.5.0

Provider-entered manual baseline + medication selection + billing-aware note · deployed 2026-06-13 · pxbuilder-aomrani.canvasmedical.com · commit 9ae3405 on aaf3215/c452945 · SDK 0.163.1
pytest 235 collected · 0 skipped · 235 mocked · 0 live live checks green after 2 live-found fixes console: 0 plugin-attributable errors 2 new fixtures (P10, P11) · additive seed 2 runtime bugs caught live, not by mocks

Highlights

  • Providers can now start tracking a patient who has no baseline on record. A "Set baseline" button above the chart opens a dialog: baseline weight, a clinical "as of" date, a required medication dropdown (Wegovy→STEP-1, Zepbound→SURMOUNT-1, Saxenda→SCALE, or Other→no band), optional counseling minutes, and a note.
  • Patients with no confirmed baseline no longer render a misleading chart. They get a friendly empty state asking for the baseline; once entered, the chart anchors to the provider-entered baseline, not the first visit weight.
  • The note lands in the patient's Note section with billing-supportive structure (baseline, medication, counseling summary, time spent), and a non-blocking CPT reminder asks whether it should also go to billing — a documentation reminder, never billing advice.
  • Backdated baselines work correctly. A patient eight weeks into therapy can have the baseline set to the therapy start date; the projection band offsets to that week-0 instead of starting at data-entry day.
  • Corrections are insurance-grade. Re-saving requires a reason, prepends a verbatim CORRECTION header to the new note, increments a revision counter, and links the superseded note — the chain of note ids is the audit trail.
  • Existing patients are untouched. The nine ZZTEST fixtures and every pre-existing patient keep their exact visit-1-baseline rendering, gated by a record-creation cutover.

Screenshots

Bugs found & fixed (both caught in live validation, not by mocks)

  • The Canvas sandbox blocks str.format. build_correction_header used TEMPLATE.format(...); RestrictedPython rejects str.format/format_map as unsafe, so only correction saves returned 500 while first saves (which build no header) succeeded — a textbook mock-green/runtime-red gap. Rebuilt with f-strings; a test pins the f-string output equal to the template render.
  • window.DiagnosticsPanel was undefined. The baseline dialog ships as a separately appended <script> and recorded its events via window.DiagnosticsPanel, but the event-log panel is a top-level const (not a window property), so the baseline-set / medication-selected / note-saved / bounds-override events were silently dropped from the v0.4 log. Exposed on window + a template-scan regression test.

Decisions & verified platform facts

AreaResolution
Storage (decision 1)PatientMetadata upsert (machine-readable: weight + clinical date + agent together) paired with a visit note (clinical/insurance documentation). Both have a sandbox remediation path; nothing synthetic enters the weight-observation pipeline.
Note path (decision 2)Note effect + PlanCommand chained via a user-set uuid4 — verified against docs and live. Note type resolved at runtime from the instance's active Encounter types (Tier-1 inventory: chronic_care_management_note, SNOMED 308335008 both present) — no chart-review fallback needed here.
Render gate (decision 3C)Earliest weight observation's record-creation time vs a cutover constant — backdated service dates can't dodge it. Provider baseline always wins; legacy patients render byte-identically.
Precedence (decision 5)Provider's dropdown is the source of truth for the band; a contradicting active medication shows a non-blocking notice + an event-log entry. P11's agreement path confirmed live (no notice).
Adult bounds scope (A4)50–1,100 lb soft bounds live only in the manual-baseline path; a test asserts the constant is referenced from nowhere a pediatric-lineage merge could reach.

Future version considerations (v0.5.1)

  • Baseline-action events live only in the session that created them (the success view tells the provider to reopen). If support reports should carry them across reopens, persist recent action events alongside the metadata.
  • Playwright's trusted click() went dead once the dialog scrolled; synthetic el.click() via evaluate was reliable — worth adding to the debug skill's Canvas-iframe notes.
  • Canvas CLI 0.166.0 upgrade still deferred (manifest pins SDK 0.163.1) — a between-cycles chore with a full suite re-run.

Tooling & skill notes

  • The seeder/rename guard pattern extended cleanly to an additive P10/P11 seeder: it refuses to start without the existing manifest, refuses to reseed a present fixture, and aborts if run before the render-gate cutover (which would grandfather P11 into legacy mode).
  • The two live-only bugs reinforce build-discipline Gate 4: this cycle's 235 mocked tests were all green while the deployed plugin 500'd on corrections. Live tiered validation is not optional.
